meaning: n.间隔,距离,幕间休息n.时间间隔
examples: The process is repeated after a short interval of time.
The interval between major earthquakes might be 200 years.
There is a 15-minute interval between two boats.
But things changed 15 minutes after the interval when the second new ball was taken.
The Tigers were beaten 26-12 by Bath on Tuesday night but were much-improved after the interval.
